claims.claim: Thermal runaway did not occur in NTM SIBs at any of the five SOCs tested (0%, 60%, 80%, 90%, 100%), whereas thermal runaway occurred in LFP LIBs at 60%, 80%, 90%, and 100% SOC but not at 0% SOC.
claims.quote: The maximum temperature rise rates (Rmax) of the NTM SIBs at five SOCs and the LFP LIB at 0 % SOC was not more than 1 ◦C/s during the heating, which indicated that the TR do not occur. However, the Rmax of the LFP LIBs at 60 %, 80 %, 90 % and 100 % SOCs was more than 1 ◦C/s and last for more than 3 s, meaning that the LFP LIBs at those SOCs experienced TR.

claims.claim: The safety valve opening temperature (Topen) of LFP LIBs was approximately 30% higher than that of NTM SIBs under the same SOC, indicating that the material inside the SIB was less thermally stable.
claims.quote: the values of Topen in the LFP LIBs were 120.4 ◦C, 118.9 ◦C, 119.0 ◦C, 116.5 ◦C, 120.0 ◦C for 0%–100% SOCs, respectively which was approximately 30 % higher than that of SIB under the same SOC. This further indicated that the material inside the SIB was less thermally stable.

claims.claim: The maximum temperature (Tmax) and maximum temperature rise rate (Rmax) of LFP LIBs were 491.4°C and 48.9°C/s respectively, far exceeding those of NTM SIBs at 167.9°C and 0.6°C/s.
claims.quote: The Tmax and Rmax of the LFP LIBs were 491.4 ◦C and 48.9 ◦C/s, respectively, which were far more than that (167.9 ◦C and 0.6 ◦C/s) of the NTM SIBs

claims.claim: At 100% SOC, the FED value for LFP LIBs (75.70186) was 28.90 times higher than that for NTM SIBs (2.53196), indicating significantly greater asphyxiation hazard from LFP LIB vent gases.
claims.quote: At 100 % SOC, the FED values of NTM SIBs and LFP LIBs were 2.53196 and 75.70186, respectively. The value for LFP LIBs is 28.90 times higher than that for NTM SIBs.

claims.claim: The maximum mass loss rate in LFP LIBs was approximately 2–7 times larger than that of NTM SIBs at the same SOC, with LFP LIB mass loss rates increasing from 11 g/s to 18 g/s as SOC increased from 0% to 100%.
claims.quote: the maximum mass loss rate in the LFP LIBs was about 2–7 times larger than that of the NTM SIBs at the same SOC, which was related to the gas release rate of the batteries. The maximum mass loss rates of the NTM SIBs were 4 g/s, 3 g/s, 2 g/s, 2 g/s, and 4 g/s while the that the LFP LIBs were 11 g/s, 13 g/s, 16 g/s, 17 g/s, and 18 g/s of from 0 % to 100 % SOC, respectively.

claims.claim: For NTM SIBs, CO is the first parameter to respond and is suitable as an early warning indicator of thermal hazard, whereas for LFP LIBs, voltage responds first and is suitable for early warning.
claims.quote: For the NTM SIB, the response order of the characteristic parameters is CO > H2 > voltage > CO2 > HF > surface temperature > EX. For the LFP LIB, the response order of characteristic parameters is voltage > surface temperature > HF > EX > CO2 > H2 > CO. Thus, for early warning parameters of thermal hazards, CO can be selected for NTM SIBs while voltage could serve for LFP LIBs.

claims.claim: The dominant gas emitted by NTM SIBs was CO2, whereas H2 accounted for the majority of released gases from LFP LIBs, with H2 in LFP LIBs reaching 5412 ppm at 100% SOC compared to a maximum of 92 ppm in NTM SIBs.
claims.quote: the dominant gas was CO2 for NTM SIB while H2 accounted for the majority of the released gases for LFP LIB... the maximum concentrations of H2 surged from 219 ppm to 5412 ppm as SOC increased, which was far larger than that for the NTM SIBs.

claims.claim: CO concentration in LFP LIBs at 60%, 80%, 90%, and 100% SOC exceeded the lethal threshold of 447 ppm, whereas CO concentrations in NTM SIBs at all SOCs (maximum 80 ppm at 100% SOC) remained significantly below the lethal threshold.
claims.quote: The maximum CO concentrations from 0 % to 100 % SOC were 48 ppm, 50 ppm, 51 ppm, 75 ppm, and 80 ppm, respectively, all of which were significantly lower than the lethal threshold... The maximum CO concentrations increased dramatically from 140 ppm to 3306 ppm, representing a 2261.43 % rise. The CO concentration at 60 %, 80 %, 90 % and 100 % SOCs were all significantly higher than the lethal threshold.
